feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: ADT : Error trying to open object of ATC remote check
entry.content:
Hi experts

Context:
We have configured the Fiori application Abap Custom Code Migration on a Hana system SXX, and successfully executed a Central check on the ECC referenced system DYY.

After configuring ADT, I'm able to see the results of the ATC check in the ATC Result Browser tab, in section "Results of Central Checks Runs.

But once in the details section, I'm facing the following issues: 

1/ Trying to open the development objects

I have the error:

2/ Trying the recheck

I have the error

 

3/ No option to open the check in ATC Problems view. 

I have no dumps in ST22, nor explicit messages in SM21.

Many thanks for your help
Regads
Pierre

entry.title: Can you provide suggestions for migrating data from SAP R/3 (4.7) to SAP S/4HANA Cloud (public)?
entry.content:
Hi ,
Aside from the staging table approach, can data migration be achieved using standard iFlows in SAP Cloud Platform Integration, or would we need to create custom iFlows or custom programs for this purpose?

Are there any other methods or third-party tools that can be used?

entry.title: Migration Cockpit - A basic guide
entry.content:
I have created this guide to show the basic steps to create and execute a project using the Migration Cockpit.  It was documented using S/4 Hana 2022 On Premise.

Configuration within each system will vary so these steps may need to be modified to address your needs but this basic scenario will help you to get started.

Migration Cockpit

Fiori App F3473 Migrate Your Data

Information on standard user roles required to complete these steps can be found in the Fiori Apps Library for the mentioned app.

 

Example Scenario

In this example I will assign the Class “CONTROLLER” to Material 000000000000000015 with value “NAV_COMM” for Characteristic “AVIONICS”

Material Classification view before migration shows there is no Class assigned currently:

 

Create Project

Create your project using Staging Tables.  This will allow for import of data from excel template files.

Migrating Data Directly from SAP System requires RFC connection set up to the source system and would be used for Extraction of data from that source system.

 

 

Select the objects in scope.  Note there may be more than one version of an object.  In the case of Classifications there is the general template and the object specific templates.  Select objects by checking the box to the left and then use the right arrow in the middle of the screen to add them to your scope. Then click on the Review button

 

Where relevant, you will receive a warning regarding predecessor objects.  It is not always necessary to include each of these in your scope as you may be managing them via alternative methods, including manually, or they may already exist – perhaps your migration project is simply to update existing objects.   Review the list and update your scope as necessary.

Review your settings, edit if necessary and then click Create Project.

 

Edit Project

It is possible to edit some of the settings you made in the Create Project step but there are also some additional settings that can be made.

From the home screen select your project and then click Edit

In General Data you can rename your project and define Custom Scope

In Migration Objects you can change the scope of objects

In CSV File Options you can manage some technical settings for the file import

In Technical Information you can define the retention period for the data in the staging tables.

 

Managing Your Project

Enter your project to begin managing each object

For each object there is a set list of steps or Actions.  Select your object and then the next Action from the dropdown menu.   Each Action is described in the following sections.

 

Download Template

There are two options for downloading the object templates:  xml and csv.  The xml file is actually presented in Excel and includes instructions, a field list (which can also be used as the basis of a field mapping document) and a separate sheet for each sub object.   These correlate to the sections in the field list, which are tables in the structure.

The csv option will provide you with a zip file containing the csv files for each of the sub objects/tables.  These correlate back to each sheet in the xml.

The xml is a little more user friendly.

In this screenshot you can see that the Header sheet in the xml is managed as a separate csv file in the master zip file. 

 

 

Upload Template

When choosing the Upload Template action, in addition to uploading your csv or xml files – or drag and dropping them – you can also review the staging table structure and review documentation about the migration object.  This is very useful information.

Drag and drop or upload your completed template.  The system will validate and notify you of issues.  Once resolved it should confirm successful import of the file.

 

Prepare

If you are using standard template files the system checks the consistency of the data in the staging tables but it will provide the option to perform this check at this point for those who imported data via another tool.

Once you choose to Prepare Staging Tables you can monitor progress via the Monitoring button in menu bar

 

 

Resolve any errors before proceeding to Mapping Tasks.  You may need to delete the uploaded data, correct the issue in the file and then re-upload.

 

Mapping Tasks

You can proceed to the Mapping Tasks step directly from the previous step as shown in previous screenshot or from the home screen for the project.

You now need to map your data :

For each attribute, confirm the source to target mapping.  The system will default the target value to be the same as the source value.  You can overwrite this if you need to.  Each value must be Confirmed, even if you are accepting the defaulted value.

 

 

Simulate

You will now perform a simulation run to validate the data load and expectations.  It can be run for all instances, random or custom selections.  Errors will be presented and must be resolved until your simulation run is clean and “Migrate” is available as the next Action.

 

Migrate

Click on the Action value “Migrate” and then choose which records to process.  Start your migration.

As before, the screen will refresh periodically to show progress but you can also choose the Monitoring button to get up to date progress.  Final status will be presented to the screen.

All data was successfully migrated and we can now check the results.

Material Classification view after migration, shows Class and Characteristic value assigned.

The Action step reverts to “Upload” to allow you to run additional files.  Note, you will need to map any new values that appear in the source data each time.  It is possible to enhance the mapping behavior of each object within a migration project using Gui transaction LTMOM which is the Migration Object Modeler.   This would be considered more of a technical task.

 

Finishing a Project

When all migration activities are completed for a project it is expected that you will mark it as Finished.  This will prevent any further activities being performed or changes being made.  It will also begin the countdown with regard to the data retention period.

If you are creating a project for ongoing upload of data then do not set it to Finished.

entry.title: How to update equipments using the Migration Cockpit app?
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

Is it possible to update equipments using the Migration Cockpit? The Migration Object "PM - Equipment" allows us to create equipments in our system, but we'd like to know if there's a way of updating the existing ones using the Migrate Your Data app.

If the update isn't possible, could you explain us why?

Thanks in advance!

Best regards,
Data migration expert team


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Will the data migration from ECC to SAP S/4 Hana include the data from link tables and the AIS?
entry.content:
I'm new to SAP migration. I would like to know if we decide to use a data migration strategy such as the SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit, which uses an RFC connection to transfer data directly from SAP ECC 6.0 to SAP S/4HANA. Is the data migration from ECC 6.0 to SAP S/4 Hana taking into account link tables like ADMI_FILES and indexed data from the archive information structure?

Thanks

entry.title: Migration of SD - Condition Records for Free Goods
entry.content:
Hi Members, 

I am migrating the condition records for free goods using the SD - Condition Records for Free Goods Migration Object in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition. During the migration, I need to input the condition table number for the condition type YA00 - Free Goods SD. However, I couldn't find the condition table number for the mentioned condition type in the system.

Then, when I simulate the migration project, it says, "For condition type YA00 and table 004, there are no valid access sequences."

I'm trying to find the KB but couldn't find any. Please help me with where I can check the condition table number and access sequence for the condition type YA00 - Free Goods SD.

Thanks.

Kenny

entry.title: Need some more clarity on SAP Note: 2545209
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am going through SAP Note 2545209 and came across one point, which is causing me confusion. Here is the solution from SAP Notes, which says as below, but the one that makes me confused here is that information structures will not change, and hence the display will work. As you can see below, 

Solution
Standard Existing Reports (enabled to read from archive) that were available in ECC and now available in S/4 can still display the FI data that was archived before the upgrade to S/4. This is because information structures will not change, and hence the display will work. However, any new report based on "HANA Live" CDS View, OData Protocol, or Ina Protocol will "not" be read from the archive.

This is applicable for all the releases in S/4.

So does it mean information structures will not migrate, thus they will remain unchanged, or does it mean they are coming as-is from old ECC 6.0, thus they are unchanged, so it will work without any issue?

If someone could provide more information, that would be great because scenarios may be different. Also, is that a green-filed or brown-filed approach? 

Thanks

entry.title: How do I extract data from SAP R/3 (4.7) ?
entry.content:
Hi,

As we are trying to migrate data from SAP R/3 (Version 4.7) to SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public) through staging tables, could you please suggest possible ways to extract data from version 4.7? We need to extract all fields and tables (both specified and unspecified/classified and non-classified) from R/3.

We have attempted to extract data using transaction code SE16N, but we couldn't extract all tables, especially the classified ones. By using transaction code RSO2 for custom data source creation, we managed to extract some data, but not all fields from the tables. Kindly suggest possible ways to achieve complete data extraction, including any other transaction codes that may be useful.

Thank you.

entry.title: Table : AUSP data is not migrated using ERP direct transfer : Characteristic
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We're using ERP direct transfer : Characteristic and Class to migrate Characteristics and Class data from ECC to S/4 in Migration cockpit(Migrate your data app). 
We've successfully migrated 2000 over records of Characteristics and 300 over records of class that have Class type = 001, Z01, Z02.

After migration, I checked related tables. CAWN and CABN have the migrated records. But AUSP does not have any entry.

How can we migrate entries to AUSP? 
Any settings are missing?

Best regards,

Kanako

entry.title: Does SAP MC DT allow for the migration of data from tables such as ZARIX and ADMI_FILES?
entry.content:
Currently, I am looking at the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit—Direct Transfer that works upon the Greenfield approach. In order to migrate the data, it requires a company code and migration object that pulls the data from a business object and that will belong to a standard table to create a project, but in my case, I need to migrate the data from the table like below:  

  • ZARIX**:  Archive Infostructures 
  • ADMI_FILES: This table stores the metadata about the archive files, such as file name, path, and status. 
  • ADMI_RUN: This table contains information about the archive runs, including the start date and times of the archive sessions, the object being archived, and the user who performed the archiving. 
  • ADMI_EXAMP : Archiving  archive file info table 

So, I need help to know whether it's possible to migrate the data from the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit—Direct Transfer approach. 

Thanks

entry.title: SAP S4 HANA - Data cleansing and duplicate check tools
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Which tools are available for data cleansing & duplicate check in SAP S/4 HANA Data migration. How can I find the license details. It would be helpful if you share your experience on using any of these quality tools?

Regards,

Radhika Mandhana

entry.title: Using Migration Cockpit for SAP S/4HANA on add module situation
entry.content:
Hello, 
I'd like to confirm about Migration Cockpit.
I've been joining a project about adding TRM module for the Customer who is already using S/4HANA FI module.
And I want to use the Migration Cockpit to add and to update BP roles in the BP master that is aliready set.
In this case, can I set BP roles, for example "issuer" and so on, for existing BP items?
If its avalable, would you tell me how to use the Migration cockpit?
Best regards.

entry.title: Error FINS_ML_MIG048 Migration cockpit converstion ECC to S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi,

In a brownfield ECC to S/4HANA conversion, we are in step M10 of the "Monitor Migration cockpit" and the error occurs FINS_ML_MIG048

We have already checked several notes, mainly the SAP note 2868953 - S/4HANA Material Ledger Migration: Improvement of error handling in Prepare package in step M10 (FINS_MIG_STATUS), but is not avaliable to S4CORE 108.

My system is S4CORE 108 (s/4hana 2023)

 

Any suggestions or experience with this error?

 

Best Regards,

Daniel Cunha.,

entry.title: How to clean up the data stored in migration temporary tables after migrating all data
entry.content:
After finishing the data migration in the Migration Cockpit app, you want to know how to clean up the temporary tables created during migration so you can free up space in the system.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Migration within Rise Program
entry.content:
Hello,

 

we are conducting a Rise with SAP project and next the Quality system will be provided and we will start with our migration project.

In OP times we had at least two clients for our migration runs ( at least 5 runs before Go Live)

Is there any information on how SAP suggest to run the project with private cloud as we will only have one client available?

entry.title: Can the Migration cockpit Template for 'Customer' be used to load both Sold-to and multiple ship-tos
entry.content:
Is it possible to use the 'Customer' (not the extend to multiple addresses or new org levels) template for Migration cockpit, so that we can load a sold-to customer and multiple Ship-to customers at the same time, and create the Sales Partner details. The aim is for all customers to be created and the Sales partner details to be updated so that the first customer is the sold-to and four other customers are Ship-to's?. When simulating and loading, we are informed that when it attempts to create the 2nd, 3rd, 4th and 5th customer (the ship-to's), the error message is given saying the the Sold-to customer (C100) does not exist in KNA1. But it should do because the first record (the sold-to customer C100, created as BP record 80) was created successful, without error, according to the log. I can use the BP transaction and view this Customer, and it looks fine, but of course none of the other 4 Ship-to's are created and the Sales Partners for the C100 are not updated either. 

Shown below is the example of the Sales Partner Tab where the sold-to customer is C100 and then we have 4 ship-to's, C100001,C100002,C100003,C100004. So I would expect all 4 customers to be created and the sales partners for C100 to have a single Sold-to and 4 ship-to's. 

If anyone can see what is wrong with this data in the template then I would relish your comments as to what might be wrong. 

 

Sales Partner tab

General data tab

BP Roles tab 

 Sales data tab

entry.title: Blank spaces in custom UI5 master detail apps in a newly migrated S4HANA launchpad.
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

One of our clients recently upgraded from ECC to the latest S4HANA on-premise system, and some of the sap ui5 apps now display a blank space between the title and list in a master page. This space disappears when I reload or minimize, but it reappears when I navigate from the home page.

I am getting the following depricated error messages in the console, but they are happening to all of my apps. If I need to update, how do I do it? These APIs are something SAP UI5 calls internally, not custom code.

I have already tried changing themes; this is not a theme-specific issue.

Changing the resource to a CDN link also failed. Please guide if there is some necessary steps needs to be done on custom ui5 apps and redeploy.

 

Thanks,

Aravind

entry.title: BP Master Migration Template
entry.content:
For a SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud setting, in transaction "BP", it is possible to set an expiration date to a BP's ID information via field "Valid to". At the same time, this field is not found within "Customer" migration XML template. How to migrate this field via Migration Cockpit in this scenario?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Migrate Your Data - Which Migration Object to use for mass creation of Articles
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

Can you please advise, which Migration Object to use for mass creation of Articles.

While using migration object 'Product' which creates the Materials and those are available in MM03. However when trying to check the same in MM43, getting error message as "You cannot use the material for retail (MAW1 missing)". 

Table MAW1 (Material Master: Default Fields and Special Retail Fields) has no entries.

Please advise.

Regards,

SureshRT

entry.title: Regarding the transfer acquisition method in LTMOM
entry.content:
We are currently considering data migration using LTMC.
Please provide guidance on the following two points.

1. Is it possible to create a project using LTMC in the development system and then transfer and import it to the validation system? 
If transfer is possible, please provide the transfer creation method. 

2. Is it possible to add items using LTMOM in the development system and then transfer and import them to the validation system? 
If transfer is possible, please provide the transfer creation method. 

Thank you & Regards,
Tsubasa 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.
